He's also considering ending work later, at 19:00 (7 PM). This extended time could provide more opportunities to earn more money and improve his rankings in both orders and earnings.

But wait, does starting earlier really guarantee better performance? It depends on several factors like traffic conditions, order distribution during the early morning, and competition from other riders who might also adjust their hours. Matthew needs to assess if there are enough orders available during that time without too much overlap with others.

Also, by working longer hours, he might face fatigue or time management issues. He needs to ensure that his stamina holds up throughout the extended period to maintain efficiency and safety while riding. Additionally, he should check if Meituan has any policies or incentives for riders who work during off-peak or late hours, which could further boost his earnings.

In summary, Matthew's decision to adjust his working hours is strategic. He aims to leverage time slots that offer higher earning potential by being among the first to pick up orders in the morning and continuing into the evening when demand remains strong. This approach should help him increase his rankings compared to other riders who might stick to standard shifts.
